compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Let `main` advance several times in a day; `rebar-autodeploy.timer` rebuilds the review-bot image on each advance (docker build layers + cache accumulate on the 30G root volume).
2. Push any change for review while a rebuild has filled the root filesystem.
3. The voter's `review_setup: clone_change_ref(...)` fails with `No space left on device` and casts `LLM-Review -1 [BLOCK — coverage-gap (review-error)]`.

Observed live 2026-07-10 ~03:44-03:55 UTC: changes 460, 461, and 462 (both patchsets + a forced /rerun) all vetoed by VOTER_ERROR disk-full. By 04:10 the filesystem was back to 27% used (the pressure was transient — a build had filled it), and `docker system df` showed 2.3GB of 100%-reclaimable build cache + 928MB reclaimable images.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: the review gate stays healthy across autodeploy rebuilds; transient disk pressure never silently vetoes reviews; an operator alarm fires before exhaustion.
- Actual: builds can transiently exhaust the 30G root volume; every in-flight review fail-closes to -1; the stuck votes never self-heal (documented /rerun is manual); nothing prunes build cache or alarms on disk headroom.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh` prunes docker build cache (and dangling images) after each successful rebuild, so cache cannot accumulate unboundedly.
- [ ] A disk-headroom check (alarm or pre-build guard) exists for the root volume, so exhaustion is caught before it vetoes reviews (extend the existing host probe/alarm seam that already watches VOTER_ERROR).
- [ ] The review-bot's clone workdir is either on the 50G `/var/gerrit` volume or documented as root-volume-resident with the headroom guard covering it.
- [ ] Runbook `infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md` gains a "disk-full VOTER_ERROR" triage entry (symptom, df/docker-system-df diagnosis, /rerun recovery).
compiled_state.title: Review-bot box: transient disk-full during autodeploy rebuild fail-closes every LLM-Review (no headroom guard, no build-cache prune)
